message:
perf(scheduler): fast-path work stealing when queue has no local tasks

Add an early check in Stealer::steal() for the common case where the
work queue contains only stealable (non-local) tasks. When
has_local_tasks() returns false, bypass locality bookkeeping, the
SmallVec of skipped locals, and the retry loop entirely — just call
steal_one_assume_non_local() directly and return.

This avoids unnecessary lock contention overhead and temporary
allocation in the hot path of work-stealing schedulers where most
queues contain globally-stealable tasks rather than thread-local ones.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
